title: قرار رئيس مجلس الادارة رقم 11 لسنة 2018: بشأن اعتماد اللائحة المحدثة لإجراءات تحويل المركبات للغاز الطبيعي

provisions.content:
, paragraph 3 of 97/23/EC(PED).1.3- Definitions:TextDefinitionProjectMeansthe Natural Gas For Vehicles Project in the Emirate of Abu DhabiUtilityMeansa company that performs a public service, subject to governmentregulationCylinderApressure receptacle/containment to store pressurized gases used for stationary,transportable and mobile applicationsCNG-CylinderAcylinder for the storage of CNGWorkingPressure pwoperationalpressure of the CNG cylinder as specified, approved and certifiedTestPressure phequals1.3×pw when tested hydraulically, and1.1×pw when tested aero staticallyUpperallowed operation temperature Twuoperationaltemperature of the CNG cylinder as specified, approved andcertifiedLowerallowed operation temperature Twloperationaltemperature of the CNG cylinder as specified, approved andcertifiedFullCyclemeansa pressure variation of more than 25% based on the working pressure of thecylinderScopeof InspectionInspectionand testing activities as specified within this guideline for the differentCNG-cylinder typesRe-inspectionPeriodindividualtime period between each CNG-Cylinders inspections as specified, approved andcertifiedExternalInspectioncoversthe assessment of the integrity of the CNG-Cylinder’s shell, including,but not limited to the presence, integrity, and function of the valves andsafety devices attached.InternalInspectioncoversthe assessment of the integrity of the internal CNG-Cylinder’sshell.StrengthTestsnormally,these tests are hydraulic overpressure pressure tests at ph to prove theresidual strength of the CNG-cylinder. Alternative test methods may be used, ifallowed and specified.CompetentInspectorAperson qualified to inspect CNG-cylinders to a limited extent only.AccreditedInspectorAperson employed and authorized by an Third Party Inspection Organizationaccredited by ESMA to inspect CNG-cylinders to the full extent.1.4- Abbreviations:Abbr. مFulltextAbbr. مFulltextASMEAmericanSociety of Mechanical EngineersNGVNaturalGas for VehiclesADR/RIDAgreementon the Transportation of Dangerous Goods on the Road/...on the railTPEDTransportablePressure Equipment DirectiveESMAEmiratesAuthority for Standardization & MetrologyUAEUnitedArab EmiratesNBICNationalBoard Inspection Code (US)CNGCompressedNatural GasMEGCMultipleElement Gas Container1.5- Reference Documents:97/23/ECPressureEquipment Directive2010/35/ECTransportablePressure Equipment DirectiveUAE.SISO IEC 17025Generalrequirements for the competence of testing and calibration laboratoriesEN16148Gascylinders - Refillable seamless steel gas cylinders - Acoustic emission testing(AT) for periodic inspectionUAE.SGSO ISO 6406GasCylinders - Seamless Steel Gas Cylinders - Periodic Inspection &TestingUAE.SGSO ISO 10460GasCylinders - Welded carbon Gas Cylinders - Periodic Inspection &TestingUAE.SGSO ISO 10461GasCylinders - Seamless Aluminium Alloy Gas Cylinders - Periodic Inspection &testingUAE.SGSO ISO 11623TransportableGas Cylinders - Periodic Inspection & TestingUAE.SISO 16923Naturalgas fuelling stations - CNG stations for fuelling vehiclesUAE.SGSO ECE R110Uniformprovisions concerning the approval of:

cylinder installation, and requalification of high pressure cylinders forthe on-board storage of natural gas as a fuel for automotive vehiclesUAEregulation for Gas Driven VehiclesHandbookNatural Gas Vehicle (NGV) conversionTROS1202TestLaboratories for the periodic inspection of CNG cylinders: Requirements andCertificationTROS1203TechnicalRules for Occupational Safety: competent inspectors1.6-Document Owner:ESMAis the owner and responsible for maintaining and enforcing this Re-InspectionGuideline.1.7- Executer Responsibilities:-Validating and controlling of the processes and sub-processes.-Review and recommendation of processes & sub-processes.-Operation and Maintenance of operational assets and to ensure integrity of theassets.-Operate and maintain all the NG Assets to optimum availability in a safe, timelyand professional manner, following the best industrial practices.1.7.1- Health Safety and Quality Department:-Responsible for Health Safety Security and Quality of operational assets and toensure integrity of the assets.-Develop and manage the Quality & Asset Integrity (AI) Policies andProcedures to ensure the wellbeing of all employees, contractors, members of thepublic and visitors to the company.-Provide the main source of advice and guidance to the company and its managerson HSSE, Quality & Asset Integrity matters.-Develop, Implement and Manage a HSSEQ Management System for the Projects in linewith Company Requirements.1.8- Method1.8.1- Types of Inspection1.8.1.1- Inspection of the state of CNG cylindersTheinspection of the state of the CNG cylinder forms part of the periodicinspection to assess, if the documentation provided is complete and consistentfocusing on tests performed previously.Duringthe periodic inspection it shall be identified, if-the type / design or the mode of operation has been changed since the lastinspection (acc, to the statement of the operator),-maintenance/repairs (acc, to the statement of the operator) were performed whichcould affect the safety of the CNG cylinder,-all required documents are available (e,g, documentation of previous performedtests like inspection before commissioning, periodic inspection and specialinspection under the responsibility of the operator),-the requirements of the extent, scope and inspection intervals were defined andmet.Ifmodifications at the CNG cylinder or at its attached equipment were made, it hasto be demonstrated by an appropriate authenticated documentation.1.8.1.2- Overall Gas System InspectionThepurpose of the overall gas system inspection is the assessment of the generalintegrity of the CNG cylinder as well as the function of the safety relatedtechnical equipment of the cylinder. Testing will normally be performed duringnormal operation.Theoverall gas system inspection covers for example:-detection of vibration, leakages, discoloration, inacceptable accumulation offoreign/solid matters/debris, external corrosion, deformation of piping andequipment-functional testing of safety related equipment/devices.1.8.1.3- External inspectionTheexternal inspection covers the assessment of the external integrity of thecylinder and its safety related equipment. The presence, integrity and functionof the required cylinders’ attached safety related equipment forms anessential part of the external inspection.Externalinspection as a visual inspection may be replaced by different appropriateequivalent methods, if the visual inspection is not useful or applicable due tothe design of the pressure equipment or due to the method of service. Ingeneral, the cylinder shall not be taken out of service for inspection. Theacceptance criteria are given in chapter 1.8.3: Acceptance Criteria for VisualInspection.1.8.1.4- Internal inspectionThepurpose of the internal inspection is to assess the integrity of thecylinder’s shell. Doing so, the range depth of inspection should be basedon potential deteriorations, while respective hints may be taken from theservice manual of the manufacturer.Generally,the internal inspection shall be performed as a visual assessment through theopening of the cylinder (cylinder valve removed), while additional appropriateinspection devices or alternative supporting test methods may be used; e.g.measuring of the wall thickness, detection of surface cracks. The cylinder orsurrounding equipment shall be removed if the condition/integrity of the shellcannot be assessed satisfactorily, or if severe damage is suspected.Allinternal inspections of the cylinders taken from a certain assembly (i.e. CNGfuelling station) shall be made before the re-inspection due date of the entireassembly.Possiblemethods and procedures of internal inspection are in example:-Visual inspection of the cylinders’ shells, if applicable with deviceslike mirrors, endoscopes, video camera systems-Appropriate non-destructive testing, such as•ultrasonic wall thickness measurement (spots or areas)•surface crack detection (dye penetration testing or magnetic particle testing)to determine cracks-ultrasonic or x-ray test methods to determine damages of the internal wallTheacceptance criteria are given in chapter 1.8.3: Acceptance Criteria for VisualInspection.1.8.1.5- Strength testingThepurpose of the strength testing is the assessment of a sufficient strength ofthe shell compared to the allowed working pressure pw.Ingeneral, the strength test shall be performed as a static pressure test withwater or another appropriate liquid. During the overpressure testing, thecylinders will be checked for craks, inacceptable deformations orleakages.Ifa liquid pressure test is not possible or not appropriate, the pressure test maybe replaced by an aerostatic pressure test with gas considering safety whileintroducing specific protection measures. Alternatively, supportingnon-destructive tests may be performed.Thetest pressure ph is-for hydraulic pressure testing: ph = 1.3x pw-for gaseous pressure testing: ph = 1.1 x pw1.8.1.6- Alternative test methodsThevarious defects and defect mechanisms may be detected using the followingmethods:DefectTestmethodcorrosion- in general-Inspection of corrosion samples
- Test of organic coating
- Test ofcathodic corrosion protectioncorrosion,erosion - reduction of wall thickness-ultrasonic wall thickness measurement
- radiographic inspectionSurfacedefects -
pinholes, cracks (on tested surface)-penetrant testing/magnetic particle test/eddy current test-surface cracksSurfacedefects -
pinholes, cracks (opposite to tested surface: internal shell)-ultrasonic testing/penetrant testing - crack testingDefects/delaminations/separationsin the shell-ultrasonic testing/penetrant testing - volume testingCreepbehavior defects - in general-baseline tests
(wall thickness, out-of-roundness, surfacemicrostructure)

elongations/strain-strain-elongation testsCyclicimpacts / fatigue

followed by NDT (Acousticemission)
- leakage testsembrittlement-hardness testingDeformation/distortion-strain testing1.8.2- Process1.8.2.1- Testing of industrial and/or automotive cylindersa-Record Cylinder number, manufacturer name, water volume, standard and all otherdetails punched on the cylinder neck.b-Cylinders forwarded to the test station for testing shall have first beendepressurized and emptied of their contents and then labeled as‘depressurized/empty’.c-The cylinder contents shall be released in a safe manner at appropriate andapproved venting areas keeping in mind dangers associated with the nature of thegas in the cylinder.d-The cylinders need to be purged. This operation should be performed using aninert gas (e.g. nitrogen), injected at a pressure of 1 bar or 2 bar, preferablywhen the cylinder is on a suitable support. It can be verified that the cylinderdoes not contain an explosive gas mixture by using a suitable measuringinstrument (e.g. a cathetometer or an explosimeter).e-Remove the cylinder valve wi
